title: Preparation of Suppressor tRNA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"63kjn","text":"This is part 3.2 of the \"A Combined Cell-Free Protein Synthesis and Fluorescence-Based Approach to Investigate GPCR Binding Properties\" collection of protocols: https://www.protocols.io/view/a-combined-cell-free-protein-synthesis-and-fluores-bqntmven","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":159},{"style":"bold","offset":160,"length":90}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":161,"length":88},{"key":1,"offset":249,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1slrg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"206s8","text":"Collection Abstract: Fluorescent labeling of de novo synthesized proteins is in particular a valuable tool for functional and structural studies of membrane proteins. In this context, we present two methods for the site-specific fluorescent labeling of difficult-to-express membrane proteins in combination with cell-free protein synthesis. The cell-free protein synthesis system is based on Chinese Hamster Ovary Cells (CHO) since this system contains endogenous membrane structures derived from the endoplasmic reticulum. These so-called microsomes enable a direct integration of membrane proteins into a biological membrane. In this protocol the first part describes the fluorescent labeling by using a precharged tRNA, loaded with a fluorescent amino acid. The second part describes the preparation of a modified aminoacyl-tRNA-synthetase and a suppressor tRNA that are applied to the CHO cell-free system to enable the incorporation of a non-canonical amino acid. The reactive group of the non-canonical amino acid is further coupled to a fluorescent dye. Both methods utilize the amber stop codon suppression technology. The successful fluorescent labeling of the model G protein-coupled receptor adenosine A2A (Adora2a) is analyzed by in-gel-fluorescence, a reporter protein assay, and confocal laser scanning microscopy (CLSM). Moreover, a ligand-dependent conformational change of the fluorescently labeled Adora2a was analyzed by bioluminescence resonance energy transfer (BRET).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5k9cm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"96n2h","text":"For Introduction and Notes, please see: https://www.protocols.io/view/a-combined-cell-free-protein-synthesis-and-fluores-bqntmven/guidelines","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":4,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":40,"length":100}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"0A0A974C873111EBB1F10A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/a-combined-cell-free-protein-synthesis-and-fluores-bqntmven"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"B49DF9C1873F11EBB4690A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/a-combined-cell-free-protein-synthesis-and-fluores-bqntmven/guidelines"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"B49DF9C1873F11EBB4690A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/a-combined-cell-free-protein-synthesis-and-fluores-bqntmven/guidelines"}}}}

steps: For specific and homogenous 3’-ends of the suppressor tRNA, an additional PCR step before transcription reaction is included. Therefore, the reverse primer contains a 2’-OMe-group to prevent unspecific nucleotides at the 3’-end of the tRNA that can be added by the T7 polymerase during transcription reaction. Amplify the template by pipetting in a PCR tube final concentrations of 1 x, Taq Buffer , 0.2 millimolar (mM), dNTP mix , 0.5 micromolar (µM), forward primer , 0.5 micromolar (µM), reverse primer , 2.5 millimolar (mM), MgCl2 , 0.01 ng/μl, plasmid and 0.04 U/μl, Taq DNA polymerase . Fill the reaction with water to a final volume of 250 µL ( see Note 5 ).

Thaw the components for in vitro transcription On ice and pipette the reaction at Room temperature . Mix 1 x, transcription buffer , 1 x, NTP mix , 1 U/μl, T7 RNA Polymerase and 8 ng/μl, template DNA .
Centrifuge the RNA at 12000 x g, °C, 1 minute und use the supernatant for the DNAseI treatment ( see Note 6 ).
Handle the TRIzol and chloroform reagent with care and use a fume hood. Add a threefold volume of TRIzol to the transcription reaction and mix carefully.
Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, at least for 01:00:00 at 4 °C and discard the supernatant.
Fold the tRNA by slowly decreasing the temperature from 80 °C to 25 °C in a PCR cycler. The tRNA can be stored at -80 °C after shock freezing in liquid nitrogen.
Incubate the reaction for 03:00:00 – 06:00:00 at 500 rpm, 37 °C, .
Add 1 U, DNAse I per 1 μg DNA .
Overlay the pellet with 1 mL, 75% ethanol for 1 mL, TRIzol and incubate for 00:30:00 at -20 °C .
Centrifuge at 7500 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes . Discard the supernatant and air dry the pellet.
Solve the pellet in water. Measure concentration using a NanoDrop and adjust the concentration to 100 micromolar (µM) .
Add 200 µL, chloroform for 1 mL, TRIzol and mix carefully for 00:00:15 by inverting.
Centrifuge at 12000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes . Isolate the aqueous phase ( see Note 7 ).
Add 500 µL, isopropyl for 1 mL, TRIzol and mix carefully.
Fill the reaction with water to the final volume of 500 µL .
creator: Satyavati Kharde
